data_quality_note: scheduled run: free sources only (HN/GitHub/Google News/Substack), no X data

items.title: Persistent context across sessions for every agent (claude-mem)
items.why_useful: Captures everything your agent does across sessions, compresses it with AI, and injects relevant context back into future sessions—making cold starts stateful. The persistent memory layer eliminates the routine problem of agents forgetting prior decisions and duplicating work on each restart. Compatible with Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, and seven other runtimes out of the box.
items.author: thedotmack

items.title: ECC: agent harness with skills, instincts, memory and security baked in
items.why_useful: A unified agent harness that bakes skills, instincts, memory, and security into the framework layer rather than bolting them on per project. The research-first development model means every pattern is validated before it ships, giving teams a reliable foundation for production Claude Code integrations. Supports Codex, OpenCode, and Cursor alongside Claude Code.
items.author: affaan-m

items.title: gstack: 23 specialized agent roles in one Claude Code setup
items.why_useful: 23 opinionated tools wired to Claude Code, each assigned a distinct organizational role—CEO, Designer, Eng Manager, Release Manager, Doc Engineer, QA. The value is a ready-made chain of command for code reviews, releases, and documentation that mirrors a real engineering team. Adoptable wholesale or cherry-picked one role at a time.
items.author: garrytan

items.title: cc-switch: single desktop app to manage Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI and others
items.why_useful: A single cross-platform desktop app that unifies Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, and Hermes Agent under one roof. Eliminates the context-switching cost of juggling separate terminals and authentication sessions when working across multiple AI CLIs. Nearly 100k stars confirms broad real-world adoption.
items.author: farion1231

items.title: Study extracted system prompts from Fable 5, Opus 4.8, and Claude Code to calibrate your own agent design
items.why_useful: A growing collection of extracted system prompts from Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Claude Code, and Claude Design alongside OpenAI, Google, and xAI models. Studying these production-grade prompts reveals how major labs structure constraints, format instructions, and guide model behavior—directly applicable when designing your own system prompts for agents. Updated regularly as new models ship.
items.author: asgeirtj

items.title: graphify: turn any codebase or schema into a queryable knowledge graph
items.why_useful: An AI coding assistant skill that turns any folder of code, SQL schemas, R scripts, shell scripts, docs, papers, images, or videos into a queryable knowledge graph. Combining app code, database schema, and infrastructure into a single graph gives agents a structured map of the full stack rather than raw files. Compatible with Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, and Gemini CLI.
items.author: safishamsi

items.title: open-design: local-first open-source Claude Design with 259+ skills
items.why_useful: A local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that runs as a native desktop app with 259+ skills and 142+ design systems. Produces web, desktop, and mobile prototypes plus slides, images, and videos with sandboxed preview and HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export. Wires into Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and 17+ other CLIs without any cloud dependency.
items.author: nexu-io

items.title: ClaudeCode GodMode: declare intent, let 15 specialized agents decide execution
items.why_useful: A self-orchestrating multi-agent system where you describe the goal and the framework decides execution: 15 specialized agents with smart routing pick the right agent for each sub-task. Parallel quality gates run verification steps simultaneously, and the skills architecture lets you extend behavior without modifying the core. Version 7.1 is explicitly optimized for Claude Fable 5.
items.author: cubetribe

items.title: Curated awesome list of Claude Fable 5 use cases with benchmark evidence
items.why_useful: A curated catalogue of Claude Fable 5 use cases organized by category, covering tutorials, integrations, demos, and benchmark evidence with source links. Useful as a discovery map when scoping what has been built and validated before committing to a new direction. 151 stars on a brand-new repo indicates rapid community uptake.
items.author: Anil-matcha

items.title: Fix: explicitly allow reasoning effort when calling Fable 5 in task pipelines
items.why_useful: Creating task pipeline runs with claude-fable-5 fails with a 400 error unless reasoning effort is explicitly allowed in the adapter configuration—this PR adds that one-line fix. The root cause and solution serve as a reference for anyone integrating Fable 5 into automated task pipelines via the Claude gateway. The fix pattern applies to any runtime adapter that needs to enable Fable 5 reasoning effort selection.
items.author: tatoalo
